plant.description: Unique History: Worshiped as a profoundly sacred tree across Hinduism and Buddhism (where Queen Maya gave birth to Gautama Buddha under an Ashoka tree). It is the unquestioned pillar of Ayurvedic gynecology. Physical Characteristics: A majestic, small evergreen tree with beautiful, dense clusters of highly fragrant, orange-yellow turning to crimson flowers. Sadly, it is categorized as highly vulnerable/endangered in the wild. Specific Chemical Compounds: Rich in tannins, catechins, flavonoids (quercetin, kaempferol), and sterols. Tannins represent its extreme astringent properties. Strict Standardizations: The most heavily adulterated plant in India (often replaced by Polyalthia longifolia). API mandates strict microscopic bark identification and tannin quantification.

ayurvedic_properties.dosha_karma: Garbhashaya Doshahara (Uterine tonic), Raktastambhaka (Hemostatic), Shothaghna (Anti-inflammatory), Vedanasthapana (Analgesic for uterine cramps).

ksheerapaka__milk_decoction_.method: Boiling Ashoka bark powder in milk and water until only milk remains. This is the absolute peak clinical method for treating heavy menstrual bleeding (Asrigdara).

ashokarishta.method: The classical fermented wine containing Ashoka, prescribed universally by Ayurvedic physicians for female reproductive stabilization.

dosage.children: Generally not applicable unless specifically directed for early-onset gynecological imbalances.
dosage.adults: Ksheerapaka Base: 5-10g bark powder. Ashokarishta: 15-30ml mixed with an equal volume of water post-meals.
dosage.elderly: Effective for managing post-menopausal bleeding or pelvic prolapse inflammation.

medicinal_uses: Supreme Uterine Tonic (Garbhashaya Doshahara): 'Ashoka' literally translates to 'remover of sorrow' for women. It stimulates the endometrium and ovarian tissues, regulating endogenous hormonal cascades to treat PCOD, heavy menstrual bleeding, and severe pelvic pain.
Anti-Hemorrhagic (Raktastambhaka): The extreme astringency rapidly constricts severely bleeding capillary vessels in the uterus, treating Menorrhagia and dysbiosis-driven Leucorrhea.
Fibroid Management: Used heavily in clinical Ayurveda to shrink uterine and ovarian cysts (Arbuda/Granthi) by physically scraping away stagnant Kapha and blocking inflammatory Pitta markers.

safety_profile.warning: GRAS Status: Recognized globally with a spotless, millennial-long safety record.
safety_profile.medical_supervision: Use under the guidance of a qualified Ayurvedic practitioner.
contraindications: Amenorrhea (absence of periods)—due to its severely astringent, flow-stopping nature, it will worsen absentee periods.
possible_side_effects: Extreme overdosing may cause severe constipation due to its intense tannins.

plant_profile.note: AI estimated profile based on known properties
